title: Running

jokes.text: Actually, I'm not sorry. I'm not sure why I just said that.
jokes.setup: Sue apologizes reflexively to someone on the phone
jokes.punchline: She immediately questions her own social autopilot behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat for Sue's hyper-competent but self-aware nature. The meta commentary on reflexive politeness is both funny and revealing.

jokes.text: I mean, I lied and everything, but it sounded true, at least.
jokes.setup: Selina celebrates her successful TV interview
jokes.punchline: She casually admits lying while praising how truthful it sounded
jokes.explanation: Classic Veep political cynicism. The casual admission of lying while celebrating authenticity is perfectly absurd political logic.

jokes.text: I can run that thing in a suit of armor.
jokes.setup: Dan suggests she should prepare for the Fun Run
jokes.punchline: Selina's absurdly overconfident boast about her running abilities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's delusional confidence, though the metaphor is somewhat obvious.

jokes.text: I'll go kiss some hateful billionaire ass on my own.
jokes.setup: Amy says she shouldn't go to the donors' brunch
jokes.punchline: Selina's brutally honest description of campaign fundraising
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the cynical reality of political fundraising with Selina's characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: Give them all donor boners.
jokes.setup: Continuing the conversation about wooing donors
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor for exciting potential campaign contributors
jokes.explanation: Typical Veep crudeness that works for character but isn't particularly clever structurally.

jokes.text: I was only, like, suicidal for a week. Huh?
jokes.setup: Suggesting the President should apologize like she did
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions being suicidal as a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures how politicians minimize personal trauma for political points.

jokes.text: He's got a cave full of bats in his skull.
jokes.setup: Explaining why POTUS won't apologize
jokes.punchline: Colorful metaphor for the President's mental state
jokes.explanation: Vivid, original metaphor that's both funny and character-appropriate for Ben's sardonic style.

jokes.text: One of Gaddafi's sons. They're polling better.
jokes.setup: Discussing potential primary challengers
jokes.punchline: Absurd comparison suggesting even dead dictator's family polls better
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that's both politically satirical and absurdist. The economy is perfect.

jokes.text: Blow the opening day pitch out of your ass?
jokes.setup: Sarcastically asking what Selina will do for an encore
jokes.punchline: Grotesque physical impossibility as metaphor for further disasters
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but relies somewhat on gross-out humor rather than pure wit.

jokes.text: We are at DEFCON fuck.
jokes.setup: Describing how bad the situation has become
jokes.punchline: Military terminology corrupted with profanity for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Effective wordplay that captures both the crisis level and Ben's style. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Sidney Purcell, talking to him, it's like being stuck in a sewer pipe filled with dead rats.
jokes.setup: Gary reading donor flash cards to prep Selina
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific metaphor for boring conversation
jokes.explanation: Vivid imagery but feels somewhat forced. Not quite Gary's usual voice.

jokes.text: This is a political prick tease today.
jokes.setup: Explaining the nature of the donor brunch
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate description of political fundraising dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but the metaphor is fairly obvious for political fundraising.

jokes.text: And we're about to meet a bunch of pricks.
jokes.setup: Following Selina's 'prick tease' comment
jokes.punchline: Gary completes the wordplay cycle
jokes.explanation: Completes the setup but is fairly predictable once Selina sets it up.

jokes.text: My campaign launchpad is going to be made of solid fucking gold.
jokes.setup: Expressing optimism about the donor brunch outcome
jokes.punchline: Absurdly grandiose metaphor for campaign success
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Selina's grandiose delusions, though the metaphor is standard.

jokes.text: Ovaries in the Oval Office!
jokes.setup: Team celebrating potential presidency
jokes.punchline: Alliterative slogan celebrating female leadership
jokes.explanation: Clever alliteration that works as both slogan and character moment, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: The West Womb!
jokes.setup: Following Amy's ovaries comment
jokes.punchline: Gary's attempt at matching the female anatomy wordplay
jokes.explanation: Gary trying to contribute but overshooting. The wordplay works but feels slightly forced.

jokes.text: Gary's awkward explanation of what a womb is
jokes.setup: Others react with confusion to 'West Womb'
jokes.punchline: Gary nervously over-explains basic female anatomy
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy showing Gary's social awkwardness when his joke fails.

jokes.text: Oh, pathetic.
jokes.setup: Gary's womb explanation
jokes.punchline: Mike's brutal assessment of Gary's failed joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work. Mike's bluntness cuts through Gary's awkwardness perfectly.

jokes.text: The Young Chungers.
jokes.setup: Discussing how Dan's playlist appeals to different demographics
jokes.punchline: Dan's portmanteau for young Danny Chung supporters
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that shows Dan's political marketing instincts, though not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's like you think in hashtags.
jokes.setup: Reacting to Dan's demographic labeling
jokes.punchline: Insightful observation about Dan's social media-driven mindset
jokes.explanation: Sharp character insight that perfectly captures modern political communication. Very quotable.

jokes.text: The Granny Chungs.
jokes.setup: Continuing his demographic naming convention
jokes.punchline: Even more forced portmanteau for elderly supporters
jokes.explanation: Weaker follow-up that shows Dan pushing the joke too far. Feels forced.

jokes.text: You're young, you're hip, you're hop.
jokes.setup: Dan asking Sue for help with the playlist
jokes.punchline: Dan's awkward attempt to sound current by literalizing 'hip-hop'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Dan trying too hard to be cool. The malapropism works well.

jokes.text: Look, I have no idea who that is, so, no.
jokes.setup: Dan suggests Jimmy Cliff for the playlist
jokes.punchline: Sue's brutally honest dismissal based on ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue moment - efficient, honest, and cutting. The economy is excellent.

jokes.text: No, no, and no.
jokes.setup: Dan suggests Elvis Costello
jokes.punchline: Sue's triple rejection for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Sue's authority, though the structure is simple.

jokes.text: I think it's in a cloud.
jokes.setup: Sue asks if he's getting songs from his iPod
jokes.punchline: Dan's vague understanding of cloud storage technology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing generational tech gap, though not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Chung will reenlist and hopefully get himself killed.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether Danny Chung is the challenger
jokes.punchline: Sue's matter-of-fact hope for Chung's death in combat
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy delivered with Sue's characteristic matter-of-fact tone. Shocking but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Duchess is down.
jokes.setup: Selina walks through glass door
jokes.punchline: Secret Service treats minor accident like assassination attempt
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between official protocol and mundane accident. The code name adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: It's not a terrorist.
jokes.setup: Secret Service treating glass door incident seriously
jokes.punchline: Amy's dry clarification of the obvious non-threat
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction to overblown security response. Amy cutting through bureaucratic nonsense.

jokes.text: Don't move. We're gonna have to cut your clothes off you.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to help with glass injuries
jokes.punchline: Dramatically inappropriate response to minor cuts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Gary's tendency to overreact and create more problems.

jokes.text: I was fine because I was drunk.
jokes.setup: Gary sharing his glass door experience
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that alcohol was his safety strategy
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark revelation delivered matter-of-factly. Good character insight into Gary's past.

jokes.text: She's stuck on a call with the Australian ambassador.
jokes.setup: Mike needs excuse for Selina's absence
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and random diplomatic cover story
jokes.explanation: Decent setup for the donor's response. Shows Mike improvising under pressure.

jokes.text: Oh, that's good. I've never heard that one before.
jokes.setup: Mike's Australian ambassador excuse
jokes.punchline: Wealthy donor's sarcastic appreciation of the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response that shows the donor seeing through political BS.

jokes.text: No, it's not, actually, but you get marks for trying.
jokes.setup: Mike says it's always a pleasure to see him
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest rejection of false pleasantries
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work for the cynical donor. Perfect balance of politeness and honesty.

jokes.text: That aspiration is to one day hope to dream together.
jokes.setup: Danny Chung giving a political speech
jokes.punchline: Meaninglessly nested aspirational language
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of empty political rhetoric. The nested aspirations are brilliantly meaningless.

jokes.text: Like catching your sister's eye at an orgy.
jokes.setup: Dan commenting on the awkwardness of both being at Chung's event
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific metaphor for uncomfortable recognition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, original metaphor that's both shocking and perfectly specific. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Well, my sister would never be at an orgy. Too uptight. She would want health records from everyone and just kill the momentum...
jokes.setup: Dan's orgy metaphor
jokes.punchline: Amy overthinking the metaphor with specific sister details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Amy's tendency to overthink. Character-appropriate but slightly verbose.

jokes.text: Don't they both die?
jokes.setup: Dan's Butch and Sundance reference for jumping together
jokes.punchline: Amy's practical concern about the film's ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy response - practical and darkly logical. Good callback to famous film reference.

jokes.text: That woman has become a living metaphor of her own career.
jokes.setup: Learning Selina walked through a glass door
jokes.punchline: Dan's insight about the symbolic nature of her accident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta observation that works on multiple levels. Perfect economy and insight into both character and show's themes.

jokes.text: I feel like one of those old used footballs that they fixed up for the kids at an orphanage.
jokes.setup: Selina describing how she feels after the accident
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor connecting her state to sad charity items
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that captures both physical and emotional state. Slightly verbose but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Unless you want to go down there and sing them a chorus of Look At Me, I'm Sandra Dee, you can't go downstairs.
jokes.setup: Selina insisting she can still meet the donors in a robe
jokes.punchline: Specific Grease reference about inappropriate attire
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that makes the point about inappropriate dress, though relies on external reference.

jokes.text: I guess we just follow the trail of her blood and Gary's tears.
jokes.setup: Looking for Selina after the accident
jokes.punchline: Pairing physical injury with Gary's emotional overreaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent pairing that captures both the physical situation and Gary's emotional nature. Great economy.

jokes.text: I think I got that off a Catholic schoolgirl once.
jokes.setup: Gary mentions St. John's wort
jokes.punchline: Mike's crude misinterpretation of the herbal supplement
jokes.explanation: Typical Mike crudeness. Works for character but relies on juvenile wordplay.

jokes.text: I'll be back.
jokes.setup: Gary talking about Russell Crowe as Gladiator
jokes.punchline: Wrong Schwarzenegger quote attributed to Russell Crowe
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gary's confused pop culture knowledge, though the joke is simple.

jokes.text: That would solve all of my validation issues with my dad.
jokes.setup: Selina asking to attend his parents' anniversary
jokes.punchline: Gary's honest admission about using Selina for family approval
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work revealing Gary's daddy issues and inappropriate relationship with boundaries.

jokes.text: God, I hope Iran drops a nuke on DC.
jokes.setup: Selina expressing frustration with her job
jokes.punchline: Extreme wish for nuclear destruction of her workplace
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that perfectly captures political frustration. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: He's like an undead cockroach with his... it's true. He's got that blank look. I bet he doesn't even have a come face.
jokes.setup: Discussing Kent surviving nuclear apocalypse
jokes.punchline: Escalating to crude speculation about his sexual expressions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character insight, though the final image is more crude than clever.

jokes.text: Can you imagine fucking that guy?
jokes.setup: Continuing the Kent discussion
jokes.punchline: Direct, inappropriate question to her staff
jokes.explanation: Creates appropriate cringe but relies more on shock than wit. Shows drug effects on her filter.

jokes.text: In the southern hemisphere, the bullshit actually flows the other way.
jokes.setup: Mike's continued Australian ambassador excuse
jokes.punchline: Clever play on water flow myth applied to political lies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay connecting geographical myth to political dishonesty. Excellent economy and wit.

jokes.text: I guess it's funny because we've all done it at one point.
jokes.setup: Learning about Selina's glass door accident
jokes.punchline: Relatable observation about universal embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor but not particularly sharp or character-specific.

jokes.text: I pushed someone through a plate glass door once. That was fucking hilarious.
jokes.setup: Following the glass door conversation
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of violent assault as comedy
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation showing the callousness of wealthy donors, though character is minimal.

jokes.text: That's your liaison d'être?
jokes.setup: Asking if Mike is liaison to the VP
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated pun on 'raison d'être' about Mike's job function
jokes.explanation: Excellent sophisticated wordplay that shows the donor's intelligence while mocking Mike's role.

jokes.text: I was born to liaise, sir.
jokes.setup: Responding to the liaison d'être pun
jokes.punchline: Mike's earnest response missing the sophistication entirely
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Mike's inability to match the donor's sophistication.

jokes.text: You know that they call me Jonad?
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about VP office treatment
jokes.punchline: Revealing his humiliating nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing both the nickname and Jonah's obliviousness to why he gets it.

jokes.text: That is tantamount to calling the President Jonad.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the nickname is offensive
jokes.punchline: Jonah's delusional equation of his importance with the President's
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah delusion. His complete misunderstanding of his importance is character gold.

jokes.text: He's the President, you're Jonad.
jokes.setup: Jonah's delusional comparison to the President
jokes.punchline: Ben's brutal reality check in simple terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and devastating put-down. Ben's directness cuts through Jonah's delusion perfectly.

jokes.text: Now go earn your name change.
jokes.setup: Ben telling Jonah to prove himself
jokes.punchline: Implication that competence might earn him back his real name
jokes.explanation: Good callback that turns the nickname into a performance metric. Classic Ben management style.

jokes.text: I got lost in the double negative, sir. What?
jokes.setup: Ben's complex instruction about drone strikes and responses
jokes.punchline: Jonah's honest confusion about basic grammar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment showing his intellectual limitations. The honesty makes it funnier.

jokes.text: She's out of her fucking mind.
jokes.setup: Gary saying Selina is completely chilled
jokes.punchline: Amy's blunt assessment of Selina's drugged state
jokes.explanation: Good Amy directness cutting through Gary's denial, though the observation is obvious.

jokes.text: Everyone's gonna find out she walked through a glass door, Dan, because she looks like she walked through a fucking glass door.
jokes.setup: Dan complaining that now everyone will know
jokes.punchline: Amy's logic about the obviousness of visible injuries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy logic cutting through political spin concerns with basic reality.

jokes.text: So are mushrooms, dipshit.
jokes.setup: Gary defending St. John's wort as natural and from earth
jokes.punchline: Amy's counter-example of natural but dangerous substances
jokes.explanation: Perfect logic and economy. Amy destroys the natural=safe fallacy with one example.

jokes.text: I'll give you all of it.
jokes.setup: Mike saying he'd take half price for his boat
jokes.punchline: Drugged Selina's immediate generous overpayment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing drug effects on judgment, though the setup is a bit convenient.

jokes.text: Look, she is fuckin' high right now.
jokes.setup: Team discussing getting Selina downstairs for press
jokes.punchline: Amy's blunt statement of the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Classic Amy directness but the observation is too obvious to be particularly funny.

jokes.text: Turning her into Julie Andrews.
jokes.setup: Explaining the drug interaction effects
jokes.punchline: Comparison to wholesome musical star
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture comparison that captures the transformation, though somewhat expected.

jokes.text: You just broke his brain, Amy.
jokes.setup: Amy explaining situation to confused Jonah
jokes.punchline: Dan's observation of Jonah's cognitive overload
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that acknowledges Jonah's limitations while advancing the comedy.

jokes.text: Jesus, look at his stupid gaping mouth. Let's put stuff in it.
jokes.setup: Jonah standing confused with mouth open
jokes.punchline: Amy's impulse to exploit his vacant expression
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Amy's cruel instincts. The visual is vivid and the impulse is perfectly mean.

jokes.text: You called me Andrew.
jokes.setup: Selina giving Jonah orders using wrong name
jokes.punchline: Jonah's confused correction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing drug effects on Selina's awareness, though fairly obvious.

jokes.text: I can even say your name the other way... Haney.
jokes.setup: Insisting she knows Jonah's name
jokes.punchline: Backwards version that's still wrong
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing her drugged logic. The backwards name is absurd but confident.

jokes.text: Gotta say, I think I handled that pretty well.
jokes.setup: After completely botching the briefing
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulation despite obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah delusion about his own competence.

jokes.text: There's something so sexy about being in a hotel in the middle of the day, right?
jokes.setup: Selina in hotel room with staff
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate observation to subordinates
jokes.explanation: Good cringe showing drug effects on professional boundaries, though not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Yep, four people to one toilet. You can hear everything that hits the water.
jokes.setup: Selina's sexy hotel comment
jokes.punchline: Dan's realistic assessment of their unglamorous situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Selina's romantic notion with harsh practical reality. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: No berets, no Stetsons, no beanies...
jokes.setup: Explaining the ring should be hat-free zone
jokes.punchline: Specific enumeration of hat types
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but the listing becomes repetitive rather than building comedy.

jokes.text: Why are you just listing hats?
jokes.setup: Amy's hat enumeration
jokes.punchline: Jonah's confused meta-observation
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where even Jonah notices the absurdity. His confusion is the perfect response.

jokes.text: Yeah, in 700 days of working with us, you've been right once.
jokes.setup: Jonah claiming to be right about something
jokes.punchline: Amy's precise statistical assessment of his track record
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy precision destroying Jonah's moment of pride. The specific number makes it funnier.

jokes.text: And, um, the only thing that's really hurt is my pride.
jokes.setup: Selina explaining her glass door accident to press
jokes.punchline: Claiming only emotional injury while clearly physically damaged
jokes.explanation: Good irony given her visible injuries, though the line is somewhat expected for political damage control.

jokes.text: Oh, no, that's a fucking wire brush to my hemorrhoids.
jokes.setup: Learning Selina said she intends to run
jokes.punchline: Grotesque metaphor for extreme discomfort
jokes.explanation: Vivid and original metaphor for political pain. Grotesque but perfectly captures Ben's reaction.

jokes.text: Fix it, or I'll liaise your balls to your desk.
jokes.setup: Ordering Jonah to fix the situation
jokes.punchline: Violent threat using Jonah's job description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that weaponizes the earlier liaison wordplay into a threat. Excellent economy and menace.

jokes.text: I love cartoons.
jokes.setup: Staff watching serious Taiwan news coverage
jokes.punchline: Selina's innocent misinterpretation of foreign news
jokes.explanation: Perfect drug-addled response showing complete disconnect from reality. The innocence makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That doesn't even look like the Leviathan.
jokes.setup: Looking at unflattering cartoon of Selina
jokes.punchline: Gary's protective criticism of artistic accuracy
jokes.explanation: Good Gary protectiveness, though the Leviathan reference feels forced rather than organic.

jokes.text: Seriously, I am totally overqualified for this job.
jokes.setup: Asked if she's up to the job of VP
jokes.punchline: Claiming overqualification while high and injured
jokes.explanation: Good irony given her current state, perfect for showing her delusional confidence.

jokes.text: I changed the 'hallelujah' to 'jalapeño.'
jokes.setup: Mike describing his karaoke performance
jokes.punchline: Specific word substitution for Mexican restaurant theme
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that shows Mike's attempt at cleverness, though the humor is somewhat obvious.

jokes.text: You're the world's biggest single-cell organism.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Jonah's inability to understand complexity
jokes.punchline: Scientific metaphor for ultimate simplicity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insult that's both scientifically accurate and devastatingly simple. Perfect Ben wit.

jokes.text: Like Sophie's Choice choices, except more important because it's gonna be about me.
jokes.setup: Describing difficult political choices ahead
jokes.punchline: Comparing to Holocaust drama but prioritizing herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina narcissism. Taking a Holocaust reference and making it about herself is peak character work.

jokes.text: He couldn't tell the difference between real glass and air either.
jokes.setup: Saying Selina looks like Marcel Marceau
jokes.punchline: Connecting mime's invisible barriers to her glass door accident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant connection between mime performance and her accident. Clever callback with perfect logic.

jokes.text: Did we warn her one of the Get Moving ambassadors was a one-legged veteran?
jokes.setup: Watching the race start
jokes.punchline: Realizing the potential PR disaster of the competition
jokes.explanation: Good setup for political disaster. The timing of the realization creates perfect tension.

jokes.text: A win-win by losing. That's good.
jokes.setup: Discussing strategy for the race
jokes.punchline: Political spin terminology for losing on purpose
jokes.explanation: Good political spin language. Captures the absurdity of needing to lose to win.

jokes.text: Oh, come on. Don't be so hard on yourself, Gary.
jokes.setup: Gary saying she's beating a disabled guy
jokes.punchline: Selina misunderstanding and thinking he's self-deprecating
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing her drugged misunderstanding. The interpretation is both wrong and cruel.

jokes.text: Burn all the White House pets and then yourselves. Burn yourselves first.
jokes.setup: Impeachment crisis requiring damage control
jokes.punchline: Escalating destruction orders with priority reversal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with dark humor. The priority reversal at the end makes it perfect Ben.

jokes.text: Those are contradictory orders.
jokes.setup: Ben giving conflicting instructions about Selina's response
jokes.punchline: Amy's logical observation about the impossibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy logic cutting through crisis confusion. Her directness is both helpful and funny.

jokes.text: What's up, you fuckin' Lorax?
jokes.setup: Gary calling for help while running
jokes.punchline: Dr. Seuss character insult based on appearance/size
jokes.explanation: Creative Jonah insult using children's book character.

jokes.text: Oh, God, this zipper is like a cheese grater on my dick.
jokes.setup: Mike running in inappropriate clothes
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor for chafing discomfort
jokes.explanation: Vivid but crude metaphor. Works for the physical comedy but relies on gross-out humor.

jokes.text: I'm surprised he gets to work without being hit by a car or punched in the mouth.
jokes.setup: Discussing Jonah's stupidity
jokes.punchline: Specific scenarios for how his idiocy should have consequences
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that imagines natural consequences for Jonah's behavior. Character-appropriate cruelty.

jokes.text: We can't have you in a photo finish with a banana.
jokes.setup: Someone in banana costume competing in race
jokes.punchline: Political implications of being photographed with fruit
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy concept. The political implications of racing against fruit is absurd but logical.

jokes.text: It's a caption contest waiting to happen.
jokes.setup: The banana race photo finish
jokes.punchline: Recognition of how media would exploit the image
jokes.explanation: Smart meta observation about media behavior. Shows understanding of how images get weaponized.

jokes.text: I smell like a hobo's craphole.
jokes.setup: Selina describing her post-race condition
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor for her smell
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective metaphor. Works for character but relies more on grossness than wit.

jokes.text: Hey, Mike, I have some sort of hazy memory of me promising to buy your boat.
jokes.setup: Selina coming down from drugs
jokes.punchline: Vague recollection of expensive promise
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing consequences of drugged decisions. The hazy memory aspect is perfect.

jokes.text: That's like trying to have sex with somebody who's passed out.
jokes.setup: Selina backing out of boat purchase made while high
jokes.punchline: Dark comparison about consent and capacity
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate comparison about taking advantage of impaired judgment. Effective metaphor.

jokes.text: What? I thought your parents were dead.
jokes.setup: Gary mentioning his parents' anniversary
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual assumption about his family status
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe showing Selina's lack of interest in Gary's personal life. Casually cruel.

jokes.text: I can't be running off to shindigs put on by the parents of people on my staff.
jokes.setup: Gary's invitation to anniversary party
jokes.punchline: Reducing family celebration to inappropriate 'shindig'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing how Selina diminishes important personal moments. The word choice is perfect.

jokes.text: I've just always assumed that they were dead.
jokes.setup: Explaining her surprise about Gary's living parents
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of morbid assumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina cruelty delivered matter-of-factly. Shows complete lack of interest in Gary's humanity.

jokes.text: I thought you were adopted.
jokes.setup: Learning Gary's parents are alive
jokes.punchline: Mike's additional wrong assumption about Gary's background
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how little anyone knows or cares about Gary's life, though less sharp than Selina's line.

tmdb_overview: Despite being told to limit public appearances, Selina continues her plans; Dan tries to play both sides of a rivalry.
insight: Campaign chaos hits 100 jokes in 43 minutes—Veep's densest episode yet.
